question_text: A country is considering three energy options: natural gas power stations, solar panels, and hydroelectric dams. Which combination would be most effective for providing reliable electricity throughout the year, and why?

options.label: A
options.text: Only natural gas because it is the cheapest option

options.label: B
options.text: Solar panels and hydroelectric dams because they are both renewable

options.label: C
options.text: Natural gas with solar panels, because solar provides energy during the day and gas provides it at night

options.label: D
options.text: Natural gas with hydroelectric dams to have both renewable and non-renewable sources

correct_answer: Natural gas with solar panels, because solar provides energy during the day and gas provides it at night
explanation: Solar panels generate electricity efficiently during daylight hours but cannot produce energy at night or on cloudy days. Natural gas power stations can be turned on quickly to provide reliable backup power when solar is unavailable. This combination ensures continuous electricity supply throughout the year. While hydroelectric dams are renewable, they depend on water availability which varies seasonally, making them less reliable as a sole backup. Using only natural gas ignores the benefits of renewable energy.
